structured_fields.name: key_pathways
structured_fields.description: Primary molecular or cellular pathways implicated in disease mechanism

structured_fields.name: biomarkers
structured_fields.description: Key biomarkers associated with disease activity or progression

structured_fields.name: disease_stages
structured_fields.description: Progressive stages of disease — LLM determines appropriate properties per stage based on disease context

items.description: Each stage object should include whatever properties best describe that stage — suggested: name, mechanism, timeline, clinical_features. Use judgment based on the disease.

example.name: Initiation
example.mechanism: Loss of self-tolerance and autoantibody production

example.name: Propagation
example.mechanism: Immune complex deposition and complement activation

freetext_fields.name: mechanism_narrative
freetext_fields.description: Rich narrative explaining the core molecular and cellular mechanisms driving the disease — how immune dysregulation, genetic factors and environmental triggers interact at a biological level

freetext_fields.name: genetics_omics_narrative
freetext_fields.description: Narrative covering relevant genetic predispositions, epigenetic factors and omics findings — integrated naturally into the pathophysiology story rather than listed separately

mermaid.instruction: If the disease mechanism has a clear directional flow (triggers → molecular events → tissue damage → clinical outcome), generate a Mermaid flowchart using graph TD direction that captures this. If the mechanism is too complex or non-linear to represent cleanly in Mermaid, omit this field entirely.

prompt_hints.tone: scientific but accessible — assume a medically literate but non-specialist audience
avoid: oversimplifying complex mechanisms to the point of inaccuracy
listing pathways without explaining their role
forcing a Mermaid diagram if the mechanism does not suit a linear flow
prefer: connecting molecular events to clinical consequences
explaining why pathways matter clinically not just naming them
using disease stage names that reflect clinical or biological reality
